title: Clarify MessageUpdateInput.reaction_changes design vs Option pattern
description: MessageUpdateInput requires reaction_changes: &'a [ReactionChange] (not Option). Callers always pass &[] or &[change, ...] explicitly (see tests 30-35). This design is correct — there's no semantic difference between 'no reactions' and 'empty list' — but a future maintainer unfamiliar with the rationale might question why it's not Option and refactor. Add a doc comment on the field explaining: 'Use an empty slice to indicate no reaction changes (equivalent to Some([])). This avoids Option wrapping since both None and Some([]) are semantically identical.' This preempts refactoring.
close_reason: Design decision: slice is intentional; reaction_changes: &'a [] vs Option<Vec<_>> trade-offs documented in .2
